methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $36M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $120M - $500M.

data.narrative:
Hystar, a Series B climate and energy technology company, has raised $36M to date. The valuation analysis triangulates across a comp universe of 9 private peers in the same sector and stage, plus 1 public comparable (LNZA).

Two methods anchor the analysis. The stage-progression approach applies a 1.9x multiplier to the $36M raised proxy, implying a $68M valuation. The peer-median method references 5 same-sector, same-stage companies—Pano AI ($500M), Sage Geosystems ($400M), Reverion ($310M), CarbonCapture Inc. ($300M), and Cemvita ($120M)—yielding a median of $310M and a range of $120M to $500M. This peer set reflects meaningful dispersion, likely driven by differences in technology maturity, capital efficiency, and market traction within the climate-tech category.

Triangulation of these two methods produces a valuation range of $58M (low) to $356M (high), with a median of $189M. The wide spread reflects structural uncertainty inherent in early-stage climate-tech valuation: peer comparables span a 4.2x range, and stage-progression methods rely on historical uplift patterns that may not hold in this capital-intensive sector. Key uncertainties include the company's unit economics relative to peers, stage and timing of next funding, and technology-risk de-risking since Series A. A valuation toward the lower quartile ($58M–$120M) would reflect a discount to peer median; a midpoint near $189M implies modest premium capture.
